items.title: 'Poop parasite' sickens hundreds statewide, Harnett County doctor warns of telltale symptoms
items.summary_raw: While Harnett County has steered clear of confirmed cases of Cyclospora, the stomach illness spreading through a parasite commonly found on produce, doctors are warning of the telltale signs of the sickness.

items.title: Smoke from fires in Minnesota, Manitoba and Ontario to create hazy sky in North Carolina this week
items.summary_raw: Air quality is forecast to be moderate through Wednesday and Thursday.

items.title: The Northeast Is Being Blanketed in Canadian Wildfire Smoke
items.summary_raw: Roughly 100 wildfires are raging out of control in Ontario, sending smoke streaming more than 1,000 miles from their origin.

items.title: Takeaways from Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing for attorney general
items.summary_raw: Todd Blanche remained composed throughout the hours-long hearing, but faced several testy moments during questioning as he seeks to win the support of all Republican senators on the committee.

items.title: Argentina is back in the World Cup final after a thrilling semifinal win over England
items.summary_raw: Argentina survived another heart-stopping match when it scored two late goals to topple England and extend the Three Lions' six-decade-long wait for another World Cup trophy.

items.title: New monkey species with orange lips found 'hiding' in DRC forest
items.summary_raw: Scientists describe "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.

items.title: The hidden skeleton “gatekeeper” inside brain cells could help fight Alzheimer's
items.summary_raw: Researchers have discovered that a microscopic skeleton inside neurons does much more than hold cells together. It acts as a gatekeeper that controls what brain cells absorb and when they absorb it. When this protective structure weakens, neurons rapidly take in harmful proteins associated with Alzheimer's disease, suggesting that stabilizing it could become a promising new strategy for preventing brain cell damage.

items.title: Investigation links Silver Alert to suspect in shuttle crash that killed 2
items.summary_raw: The man alleged to have run a red light causing a deadly crash with a shuttle bus near Raleigh-Durham International Airport had himself been reported missing days earlier.

items.title: NC Zoo historic marker honors Astrochinp Ham
items.summary_raw: A new North Carolina highway historical marker near the N.C. Zoo honors Ham, the first chimpanzee in space, and recognizes his pioneering 1961 mission and final years living with other chimpanzees in Asheboro.

items.title: How To Watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup Finals: Spain vs. Argentina
items.summary_raw: The end of the FIFA Men’s World Cup is nigh. Here’s how to watch the final games and the first ever World Cup halftime show.

items.summary_raw: The New York Times journalist Jonathan Swan says the president is fixated on becoming a "great man of history" during his second term. Swan's new book, written with Maggie Haberman, is Regime Change.

items.summary_raw: A large section of the giant Morandi motorway bridge crumbled and collapsed on to the railway tracks below.

items.title: This pet gecko could help scientists unlock the secrets of cancer
items.summary_raw: An unusual leopard gecko that naturally develops aggressive tumors may become an important new model for cancer research. Scientists found its tumors share key genetic changes with human cancers, offering a rare opportunity to study the disease as it develops naturally.

items.title: Weldon man shot and killed in shooting, Halifax County deputies investigating
items.summary_raw: A man was shot and killed in a shooting Saturday night near the Dogwood Park Apartments in Halifax County.

items.title: Drought conditions make hot weather even hotter
items.summary_raw: While the lack of rainfall is an obvious part of North Carolina's ongoing drought, there's another side effect that many people don't realize. Drought conditions can make hot weather even hotter.

items.summary_raw: The Trump administration has not disclosed its cost estimates for the Iran war.

items.summary_raw: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche will be on Capitol Hill Wednesday for his high-stakes confirmation hearing to lead the Justice Department permanently.

items.title: Human Rights Watch report details abuse at immigration detention center
items.summary_raw: People held in the immigration detention camp at the Fort Bliss military base in Texas have experienced beatings and life-threatening medical neglect, according to a report by Human Rights Watch.

items.title: Argentina face fine for Falklands banner in semi-final win
items.summary_raw: Argentina face the prospect of a Fifa fine after their players celebrate the World Cup semi-final win against England with a banner in support of their country's claims to the Falkland Islands.

items.title: NASA captured the Black Sea turning brilliant turquoise from space
items.summary_raw: NASA's PACE satellite captured the Black Sea glowing turquoise during its annual phytoplankton bloom. The vivid color comes from massive numbers of coccolithophores, microscopic organisms whose reflective shells brighten the water enough to be seen from space. An astronaut aboard the International Space Station also photographed the bloom spreading through the Bosphorus, revealing swirling currents.

items.title: Yard Sale for Africa
items.summary_raw: Yard sales twice a month in Apex raise money for children in South Africa.

items.summary_raw: Since WIRED reported on Meta’s NameTag face recognition system, company executives have made confusing and conflicting remarks about its very existence.

items.title: US military to start testosterone testing, Hegseth says
items.summary_raw: "We owe our warriors the absolute best medical care in the world, and this program delivers on that obligation," Hegseth said in a video.

items.title: Archaeologists found Homer's Iliad inside a 1,600-year-old Egyptian mummy
items.summary_raw: A 1,600-year-old mummy discovered in Egypt has revealed something archaeologists had never seen before: a fragment of Homer’s Iliad used during embalming. The papyrus was identified as part of the famous "Catalogue of Ships," one of the best-known sections of the ancient epic. Researchers say it is the first archaeological evidence of a Greek literary text being intentionally incorporated into the mummification process, adding a fascinating new chapter to the history of both literature and ancient burial customs.

items.title: 'There are best practices' | Evaluating courthouse safety, and federal efforts to improve it
items.summary_raw: A US Senate bill aims to improve safety for state court systems, including better education for security and improved threat reporting

items.title: AI Isn’t Smarter Than a Baby—Yet
items.summary_raw: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.

items.title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz faced questions in Senate hearing
items.summary_raw: Trump's nominee faces the Senate health committee for her confirmation hearing on July 15. If confirmed, she will lead an agency dealing with workforce and leadership shortages and new layers of political review.

items.title: Todd Blanche's high-stakes confirmation hearing for attorney general is underway
items.summary_raw: President Trump's former personal attorney is under the microscope on Capitol Hill. Lawmakers on the Senate Judiciary Committee are questioning Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, whom Trump has nominated for the job permanently.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'
items.summary_raw: US President Donald Trump says he is yet to decide whether or not he will "finish off" Iran.

items.title: Are humans really the ultimate super-predator?
items.summary_raw: Humans are often described as the planet's ultimate "super-predator," but wild animals do not fear every human the same way. After analyzing three decades of research, scientists found that animals become much more alert and spend less time feeding when people pose a real threat, such as hunters or fishers. In contrast, tourists, researchers, and other non-lethal humans trigger far weaker and less predictable reactions.

items.title: Cyclospora source still unknown in North Carolina despite Michigan lettuce lead
items.summary_raw: As Michigan narrows its Cyclospora investigation to lettuce, North Carolina experts say the state's more than 300 cases have not yet been linked to a single food source.

items.title: Thinking Machines Lab Drops Its First Model
items.summary_raw: Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter open source model, was trained to understand video and audio. It could help Thinking Machines establish itself among competitors like Anthropic and OpenAI.

items.title: Todd Blanche faces high-stakes confirmation hearing for attorney general
items.summary_raw: Blanche won Senate confirmation early in President Trump's second term to serve as the No. 2 official at the Justice Department. Now he's facing a contentious hearing as he seeks the top job.

items.title: Democrats' witness opposing Blanche's confirmation is more than an Epstein survivor
items.summary_raw: Dani Bensky and a group of women who were abused by Jeffrey Epstein have formed a bond they call a "survivor sisterhood." They live in two worlds, of advocacy and everyday life and motherhood.

items.title: Russian attacks kill 14 as Ukraine hits Black Sea oil tankers
items.summary_raw: Civilian casualties are reported in a number of Ukrainian regions, while Kyiv hits 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea.

items.title: NASA selects 41 space technologies for future Moon and Mars exploration
items.summary_raw: NASA has chosen 41 commercial technology projects that could solve critical challenges for future missions to the Moon and Mars. From powering lunar outposts to protecting spacecraft from Moon dust, the innovations are designed to push both space exploration and the commercial space economy forward.

items.title: Killed in the crosswalk; man killed on Atlantic Ave. intersection without operating traffic signals
items.summary_raw: After a deadly pedestrian crash on Atlantic Avenue, WRAL Investigates found that newly installed traffic and crosswalk signals at the intersection are still not operating, raising questions about pedestrian safety.

items.title: Lionel Messi’s Final World Cup—and the Death of Early Retirement
items.summary_raw: Argentina’s Lionel Messi was supposed to be done years ago. Now, sports science is helping soccer’s biggest stars rewrite the rules of aging.

items.summary_raw: The speech comes as President Trump has escalated calls for Republicans to pass tighter federal voting rules for November's midterm elections.

items.summary_raw: Blanche won Senate confirmation early in President Trump's second term to serve as the No. 2 official at the Justice Department. Now he's facing a contentious hearing as he seeks the top job.

items.title: What if Bluey spoke one of the world's oldest living languages?
items.summary_raw: Five episodes of the award-winning children’s cartoon have been released in Australian Indigenous language for the first time.

items.title: Astronomers just found four hidden white dwarf stars near Earth
items.summary_raw: Four nearby white dwarf stars have been discovered hiding in plain sight beside brighter red dwarf companions. Hubble's ultraviolet observations finally revealed the long-hidden stellar remnants, including one just 25 light-years away that took nearly three decades to confirm. The findings match long-standing predictions and suggest our corner of the galaxy may contain many more undiscovered white dwarf binaries.

items.title: Carowinds' Fury 325 coaster closed for 2nd time in 3 years
items.summary_raw: Carowinds closed its popular roller coaster, Fury 325, on July 7 after identifying an issue with the ride's foundation during a routine maintenance inspection.

items.title: An Inventor of Apple’s FaceID Wants to Analyze Your Brain’s Health With AI
items.summary_raw: Gidi Littwin’s new AI startup, Hemispheric, makes diagnostic brain scans for conditions like depression, PTSD, and Parkinson’s. He wants the technology to be as cheap and easy as for a blood test.

items.title: Gov. Hochul orders a pause on data centers in New York for up to a year
items.summary_raw: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an order pausing construction of large data centers — the first statewide pause in the country.

items.title: Baltimore to overhaul how it responds to calls for mental health and other services
items.summary_raw: Baltimore is setting up a fourth branch of first responders in addition to fire, police and EMS. They will handle calls for people needing mental health support, but also connection to city services.

items.title: French MPs approve assisted dying law with strict rules after years of argument
items.summary_raw: The bill would allow assisted dying for terminally ill adults who meet strict criteria.

items.title: Going to museums, movies, and theater may help your body stay younger
items.summary_raw: Going to the cinema, visiting museums, or attending concerts may do more than entertain. Researchers found that older adults who regularly participated in cultural activities tended to have bodies that functioned like those of people about three years younger. They believe stronger social ties, better mental health, and healthier habits could help explain the link, although more research is needed to determine whether cultural activities directly slow aging.

items.title: Fact-check: Trump says illegal immigration caused 40% rise in housing costs
items.summary_raw: With affordability in the spotlight, President Donald Trump and his administration have sought to tie rising housing costs to illegal immigration under his predecessor, President Joe Biden.

items.summary_raw: Apple just raised its prices. Here’s an updated breakdown of the brand’s tablets to find the best one for your needs.

items.title: In the aftermath of deadly shootings, ICE pauses most traffic stops
items.summary_raw: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement will pause non-urgent vehicle stops after two deadly shootings in less than a week, Maine U.S. Sen. Angus King's office tells NPR.

items.title: Trump administration's proposal to shift management of grizzly bears causes confusion
items.summary_raw: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum says he's turning management of threatened grizzly bears in states that share Yellowstone National Park to state governments. What exactly that means is not yet clear.

items.title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
items.summary_raw: There is more confidence in Xi Jinping than Donald Trump, the US think tank's survey indicates.

items.title: Scientists finally solved why some frogs survive a deadly fungus
items.summary_raw: A deadly fungus has wiped out amphibian populations around the globe, yet some mysteriously recover. Researchers discovered that survivors develop powerful immune defenses while they are still tadpoles, giving them a head start before the fungus can attack after metamorphosis. The study also uncovered a vast collection of previously unknown antimicrobial peptides that could someday inspire new drugs to fight infections.

items.title: Child dies after drowning in Durham Tuesday night
items.summary_raw: Durham County EMS confirmed to WRAL they responded to the reported drowning involving a child. That child was transported to Duke University Hospital but did not survive.

items.summary_raw: But with great power comes a great price.

items.summary_raw: The New Yorker writer Jonathan Blitzer says thousands of people are being held in tents in the El Paso desert, where inhumane conditions have become a tool to pressure people to accept deportation.

items.title: How the federal government is responding to the worsening western fire season
items.summary_raw: The bad summer fire season predicted for Western states is in full swing, with more and larger fires predicted. Federal officials say they have adequate resources to fight them, for now.

items.title: Five arrested after Hong Kong police raid independent bookshops
items.summary_raw: Officials say they are suspected of selling "seditious" books which incited "hatred" against authorities.

items.title: Why have humans collected crystals for 780,000 years? Chimpanzees may hold the answer
items.summary_raw: Chimpanzees showed a remarkable attraction to crystals, choosing them over ordinary stones and studying them with intense curiosity. The results suggest that the same unusual features may have fascinated early humans long before crystals had any practical use.

items.summary_raw: Jackson's office estimates its recommendations would save customers $960 million over two years while allowing Duke Energy Progress to make needed grid investments.

items.summary_raw: Make some noise for safety with our favorite alarms, from keychain sirens to wearable bracelets.

items.summary_raw: The March and March provincial leader died of his injuries in hospital several days after being shot.

items.title: Chewing bubble gum after beetroot may help lower blood pressure
items.summary_raw: A new study found that chewing sugary gum after eating nitrate-rich vegetables or drinking beetroot juice helped the body produce more beneficial nitrite, leading to a temporary drop in blood pressure. Researchers hope the discovery will lead to healthier ways to enhance the cardiovascular and performance benefits of dietary nitrate without relying on sugar.

items.title: Carowind's Fury 325 closes for foundation issues
items.summary_raw: During a routine maintence inspection, Carowinds found an issue with the foundation of their rollercoast, Fury 325. A date for reopening the coaster has yet to be announced.

items.summary_raw: CN Rail told the BBC that operations were temporarily suspended in the affected area in the province of Ontario.

items.title: Eating chili peppers may raise the risk of one deadly cancer
items.summary_raw: A major review found that people who consumed the most chili peppers had a substantially higher risk of esophageal cancer, though the evidence was less clear for stomach and colorectal cancers. Researchers emphasize that the findings show an association, not proof of cause and effect, and that more research is needed to determine whether moderate consumption carries similar risks.

items.title: My Ebike Delivery Went Missing. When I Tried to Recover It, I Ended Up in Chatbot Hell
items.summary_raw: Companies’ increasing reliance on AI chatbots isn’t making the customer service experience smarter. It’s just making it more infuriating.

items.title: Seven Britons among those killed in Spain wildfires
items.summary_raw: Twelve of the 13 victims were foreign nationals, Spanish authorities said on Tuesday.

items.title: Are there aliens on exoplanet K2-18b? Scientists just scanned it for signals
items.summary_raw: K2-18b is one of the most promising worlds for the search for extraterrestrial life, so astronomers conducted an unusually powerful radio survey using both the VLA and MeerKAT telescopes. Advanced software analyzed millions of signals, filtering out Earth-based interference and other false positives. No convincing artificial radio transmissions were found, but the project demonstrated a powerful new approach that will make future SETI searches faster and far more effective.

items.title: The Explosive Diarrhea Outbreak Is About to Get Much Bigger
items.summary_raw: Official case counts likely capture only a fraction of US cyclosporiasis infections, and the outbreak is likely to get worse before it gets better.

items.title: Venezuela government to launch formal talks with opposition members
items.summary_raw: The opposition says the aim of the talks is to strengthen the democratic institutions and the electoral system.

items.title: A 37-year soil experiment revealed a hidden climate threat
items.summary_raw: The world's longest-running soil warming experiment has revealed an unexpected climate concern. After nearly four decades, researchers found that warming can cause microbes to break down stable soil carbon that scientists once believed was largely protected. That releases extra carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, potentially accelerating global warming.

items.title: OpenAI Staffers Are Funding a Rival Super PAC to Take on Their Boss
items.summary_raw: OpenAI employees have donated more than $215,000 to a political effort opposing Leading the Future, a group backed by the company’s president, Greg Brockman.

items.title: Italy's Meloni suffers surprise setback in close vote on electoral reform
items.summary_raw: The defeat triggered opposition calls for Meloni to resign ahead of next year's general election.

items.title: Popular weight-loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy may slow biological aging
items.summary_raw: Researchers found that semaglutide, the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy, slowed biological aging markers in adults with HIV, marking the first clinical evidence that the drug may influence human aging. Although the findings are encouraging, scientists say larger studies are needed before concluding that the medication can help people age more slowly.
